heroHeadline: Day 57: Fractura Total Day 9 — CJNG Monstruos Armored Vehicles Patrol Jalisco-Zacatecas Border as Both Factions Hold Buildup Phase
heroSubtitle: Day 57 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco (February 22, 2026). The CJNG fractura total — the open internal war between El Jardinero/El Sapo and O3/Los Cuinis (declared April 11) — is now 9 days old. Both factions remain in a sustained buildup phase with no direct inter-faction combat confirmed in the CJNG Jalisco heartland. The week's most significant field development: CJNG 'Monstruos' (improvised steel-armored vehicles) deployed Saturday evening (April 18) in the Colotlán area on the Jalisco-Zacatecas border, with an armed clash reported in Laurel, Tepetongo municipality (Zacatecas) at approximately 7:20 PM. The convoy — consistent with El Jardinero faction territorial positioning — circulated across six northern Jalisco-Zacatecas border municipalities. No casualties confirmed. On the enforcement front: Operativo Enjambre cumulative total has crossed 80+ official arrests; El Salo's April 17 Tecate arrest reflects continued CJNG expansion via Sinaloa Cartel defectors under post-Mencho fragmentation. Analysis milestone approaching: ACLED scheduled a live webinar, 'After El Mencho: CJNG Succession Dynamics and the Future of Security in Mexico,' for April 23 — the first major international analytical event dedicated to post-Mencho succession dynamics, featuring InSight Crime's Chris Dalby, Victoria Dittmar, and Sandra Pellegrini. FIFA World Cup 2026: 52 days to the opening match (June 11, Mexico City). Guadalajara's Akron Stadium hosts four group-stage matches.
footerNote: Data sourced from SEDENA, SSPC, FGR, DEA, U.S. DOJ, NCTC, U.S. Treasury (OFAC), InSight Crime, Crisis Group, Wilson Center, Reuters, AP, Wall Street Journal, El País, Washington Post, France 24, CGTN, Al Jazeera, Latin Times, Infobae México, La Silla Rota, WOLA, Milenio, Excélsior, Proceso, El Financiero, El Imparcial, Borderland Beat, RED Michoacán, Semanario ZETA, La Voz de la Frontera, TV Azteca Baja California, and major Mexican news outlets. All contested figures noted. Last update: April 20, 2026.
